{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Komagata-jinja (Oshu) torii.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of [[Rikuchū Province]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the [[Hakone]] region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times [[Horses in warfare|war horses]] were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[industrial development]] and [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with [[Academic achievement|exam success]] and [[home safety]]. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Guanyin|Kannon]] and [[Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]]. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Guanyin|Kannon]] and [[Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]]. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Komagata-jinja (Oshu) torii.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of [[Rikuchū Province]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the [[Hakone]] region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times [[Horses in warfare|war horses]] were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[industrial development]] and [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with [[Academic achievement|exam success]] and [[home safety]]. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />

The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />

{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Komagata-jinja (Oshu) torii.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of [[Rikuchū Province]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the [[Hakone]] region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times [[Horses in warfare|war horses]] were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[industrial development]] and [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with [[Academic achievement|exam success]] and [[home safety]]. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Guanyin|Kannon]] and [[Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]]. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
== References == {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Komagata-jinja (Oshu) torii.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of [[Rikuchū Province]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the [[Hakone]] region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times [[Horses in warfare|war horses]] were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[industrial development]] and [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with [[Academic achievement|exam success]] and [[home safety]]. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Guanyin|Kannon]] and [[Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]]. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
== References == {{reflist}}

It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />

== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Komagata-jinja (Oshu) torii.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of [[Rikuchū Province]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the [[Hakone]] region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times [[Horses in warfare|war horses]] were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[industrial development]] and [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with [[Academic achievement|exam success]] and [[home safety]]. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Guanyin|Kannon]] and [[Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]]. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Guanyin|Kannon]] and [[Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]]. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Komagata-jinja (Oshu) torii.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of [[Rikuchū Province]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the [[Hakone]] region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times [[Horses in warfare|war horses]] were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[industrial development]] and [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with [[Academic achievement|exam success]] and [[home safety]]. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />

== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Guanyin|Kannon]] and [[Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]]. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Komagata-jinja (Oshu) torii.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of [[Rikuchū Province]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the [[Hakone]] region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times [[Horses in warfare|war horses]] were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[industrial development]] and [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with [[Academic achievement|exam success]] and [[home safety]]. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Guanyin|Kannon]] and [[Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]]. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[industrial development]] and [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with [[Academic achievement|exam success]] and [[home safety]]. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Guanyin|Kannon]] and [[Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]]. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
== References == {{reflist}} {{coord|35.18746|139.02162|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinmei shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \
{{Ichinomiya}}

（[[天照大御神]]・[[天之常立神|天常立尊]]・[[国狭槌尊|国狭立尊]]・[[アメノオシホミミ|吾勝尊]]・置瀬尊・[[ホオリ|彦火尊]]の6柱の総称） |神体 = 駒ヶ岳（[[神体山]]） |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[国幣小社]]
[[別表神社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月19日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map+|Japan Iwate|width=210|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Iwate|lat_deg=39|lat_min=8|lat_sec=11.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=8|lon_sec=17.1|position=bottom|label=本社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Iwate|lat_deg=39|lat_min=11|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=140|lon_min=55|lon_sec=11.4|position=left|label=奥宮|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Iwate|lat_deg=39|lat_min=11|lat_sec=8.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=3|lon_sec=42.7|position=top|label=里宮|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|39.162461|N|141.028618|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=10|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|39|8|11.5|N|141|8|17.1|E}}|label1=本社|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|39|11|43.8|N|140|55|11.4|E}}|label2=奥宮|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8|mark-coord3={{coord|39|11|8.9|N|141|3|42.7|E}}|label3=里宮|label-pos3=bottom|mark-size3=8}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''駒形神社'''（こまがたじんじゃ）は、[[岩手県]]にある[[神社]]。[[奥州市]]水沢中上野町の本社、[[胆沢郡]][[金ケ崎町]]西根の駒ヶ岳山頂の奥宮、胆沢郡金ケ崎町西根雛子沢の里宮からなる。[[式内社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[国幣小社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の6柱で、「'''駒形大神'''」と総称される神社由緒書。。 * [[天照大御神]] （あまてらすおおみかみ） * [[天之常立神|天常立尊]] （あめのとこたちのみこと） * [[国狭槌尊|国狭立尊]] （くにのさたちのみこと） * [[アメノオシホミミ|吾勝尊]] （あかつのみこと） * [[邇邇芸命|置瀬尊]] （おきせのみこと） * [[ホオリ|彦火尊]] （ひこほのみこと） 当社は駒ヶ岳の神霊を祀ったものとされるが、古くよりその神霊を人格神に比定する諸説が挙げられている。現在の6柱とする説は、雛子沢里宮の[[寛政]]9年（[[1797年]]）棟札や、[[仙台藩]]編纂の『安永風土記』に記載が見られる。これらは、中近世の神仏混交期において駒ヶ岳外輪山を各天神地祇に擬したことに由来すると見られている（最高峰の大日岳に天照大神、第2の駒ヶ岳に天照大神の子の吾勝尊、など）{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。以上のほか、人格神を[[ウガノミタマ|宇賀御魂大神]]・[[天照大神]]・[[アメノオシホミミ|天忍穂耳尊]]とする説、[[上毛野氏|毛野氏]]の祖神とする説等がある{{Sfn|駒形神社（平凡社）|1990年}}。 祭神の駒形神は[[ウマ|馬]]の守護神とされ、[[馬頭観音]]や[[大日如来]]と習合し、東日本の各地に[[勧請]]されて信仰されている{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。馬の守護神とされた背景は、古代に付近一帯が[[軍馬]]の産地であったことが考えられている{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。 == 歴史 == 当社は駒ヶ岳（[[焼石駒ヶ岳]]/駒形山）を祀る神社として、明治以前は駒ヶ岳山頂の本宮（奥宮）、[[北上市]]和賀町岩崎と[[金ケ崎町]]西根雛小沢の各里宮をして奉斎された{{Sfn|駒形神社（角川）|1985年}}。水沢の現在社（水沢本社）は[[明治]]36年（[[1903年]]）の新設である{{Sfn|駒形神社（平凡社）|1990年}}。それ以前の水沢本社の地は[[鹽竈神社 (奥州市)|鹽竈神社]]（現・境内別宮）の境内地であった{{Sfn|駒形神社（平凡社）|1990年}}。以下、駒形神社一般の歴史について概説する。 === 創建 === 創建は不詳で、様々な伝承が現在に伝えられている{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。社伝の1つでは、[[雄略天皇]]（第21代）21年に、[[籠神社]]（[[京都府]][[宮津市]]）から[[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂大神]]を勧請して山頂に祀り、里宮に[[オオゲツヒメ|大宜津比売神]]と[[事代主|事代主神]]を配祀したが、のちに前記6柱となったという{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。 別伝では、[[景行天皇]]（第12代）40年に[[日本武尊]]が東征に際し、[[蝦夷]]平定のために前記6柱を勧請・創建したという{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。また、[[坂上田村麻呂]]が当地で倒れた愛馬を祀ったが、のちに慈覚大師（[[円仁]]）が廻国した際にその駒形神を駒ヶ岳山頂に移して本宮を造営、さらに[[源義家]]が[[前九年の役]]の際に戦勝祈願をしたともいう{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。 別説として、上毛野（のちの[[上野国]]、現・[[群馬県]]）を根拠とする[[上毛野氏]]一族が当地に来住するにあたり、駒ヶ岳を上毛野氏氏神の[[赤城山]]（[[赤城神社]]）に擬して奉斎したとする説もある{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。その中で、休火山である赤城山の外輪山に「駒形山」が存在することから、毛野氏が上毛野氏と下毛野氏に分かれた後にそれぞれ勢力を北に伸ばし、外輪山を持つ山の中で二番目の高峰を赤城山になぞらえて「駒ヶ岳」または「駒形山」と名付け、駒形大神を祀ったとする[http://www.rnac.ne.jp/~komagata/page002.html 「駒形神社御由緒」（駒形神社公式ホームページ）]。そして[[雄略天皇]]の時代に、上毛野氏が奥州において現在の「駒ヶ岳」を見出して名づけ、山頂に駒形大神を勧請したのが始まりであるとするものである。この説に従えば、駒形神社の祭神は赤城山の神と同一か、深い関連を持つ存在ということになる。関連して、『[[続日本後紀]]』[[承和 (日本)|承和]]8年（[[841年]]）3月2日条には江刺郡擬大領として「上毛野胆沢公毛人」の名が見え、上毛野氏と胆沢との関わりが指摘される{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。 さらに赤城神社との関連では、源実朝が『金塊和歌集』において赤城神社を「からやしろ」と詠んでいることから、赤城神社は「から」すなわち中国や韓国に由来するともされるが、当時の朝鮮にあった高麗が「コマ」と呼ばれたことから、駒形という名称は、「高麗唐」すなわち「コマカラ」が「こまかた」のち「こまがた」に転訛したともいう。関連して箱根神社の摂社である駒形神社では、朝鮮から高麗大神が勧請されたとしている。 なお奥宮は現在駒ヶ岳に鎮座するが、元々は駒ヶ岳南方で最高峰の大日岳（経塚山）山頂にあったとする伝承がある{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。 === 概史 === [[六国史|国史]]では[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）に「駒形神」の[[神階]]が正五位下に、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]4年（[[862年]]）に従四位下に昇叙された旨の記事が見える{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。従四位下の神階は[[陸奥国]]内で最高位になる（陸奥国内で従四位下は計9社陸奥国で六国史終了時点で従四位下まで進んだ神社としては、駒形神社のほか、苅田嶺神社（論社複数）、[[鹽竈神社|志波彦神社]]、[[零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|零羊埼神社]]、[[拝幣志神社]]、計仙麻神社（論社複数）、[[日高見神社 (石巻市)|日高見神社]]、志波姫神社（論社複数）、[[磐椅神社]]の計9社がある。なお東北地方で見た場合には、[[出羽国]]において[[大物忌神社]]と[[月山神社]]が各々従二位に叙せられている。）[http://www.ganshinsei.jp/ousyuushi/371a.html 「駒形神社」（岩手県神道青年会ホームページ）]。駒形神社側ではこの神階について、駒形神社への坂上田村麻呂の崇敬が篤かった関係で、胆沢の鎮守府から神階を高くすべきとの申し出が何度もあったためと説明している。 また、[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では陸奥国[[胆沢郡]]に「駒形神社」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列している{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。駒形神社側の見解では、全国の駒形神社の中で当社と宮城県栗原郡の駒形根神社の2社のみが神名帳に記載があるという。 平安時代頃には、上記の坂上田村麻呂のほか、源頼義・義家や[[平泉]]の藤原氏の崇敬も篤かったと伝える。箱根神社の縁起には、藤原秀衡が銅を鋳て駒形の神の像を作り祀るなど、藤原氏の駒形大神への信仰の篤さが読み取れる記載があるという。 [[江戸時代]]の時点では、駒ヶ岳は[[仙台藩]]と[[盛岡藩]]の境界であり、里宮はそれぞれの藩内に1社ずつがあった{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。また、山頂の本宮（奥宮）は両藩によって20年目ごとに交互に建て替えがなされていた{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）、山頂の本宮（奥宮）が[[近代社格制度]]において[[国幣小社]]に列するにあたり、本宮・里宮とも参拝に不便であるとして、当時の[[水沢県]]県庁に近い鹽竈神社の本殿が仮遥拝所となされた（鹽竈神社は境内社の春日神社に遷座）{{Sfn|駒形神社（平凡社）|1990年}}。そして明治7年（[[1874年]]）、社殿が改修されて正式な遥拝所とされた。明治36年（[[1903年]]）に山頂の神霊が遥拝所に遷され、元は鹽竈神社のものであった社殿等一切は駒形神社に編入された。鹽竈神社は境内別宮であった春日神社に合祀され、社名が「春日神社」から「鹽竈神社」に改称されることとなった。 戦後は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に列し、近年では[[陸中国]][[一宮]]とも称されている。 2010年（平成22年）8月1日、山頂の奥宮にある老朽化した社殿を解体し、新しく社殿を造営した[http://jinja.blog5.fc2.com/blog-entry-465.html 「8月1日、奥宮竣工祭、登拝祭」駒形神社なんでも綴り（駒形神社宮司・山下明のブログ）]。 === 神階 === * [[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）9月2日、正五位下 （『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』） - 表記は「駒形神」。 * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]4年（[[862年]]）6月18日、正五位下から従四位下 （『[[日本三代実録]]』） - 表記は「駒形神」。 == 境内 == === 本社 === 境内と[[水沢公園]]はヒガンザクラ系の桜の名所として知られ、老木の樹齢は250年から300年にも及ぶ{{Sfn|駒形神社（平凡社）|1990年}}[http://www.bunka.pref.iwate.jp/rekishi/bunkazai/index.html 「駒形神社ならびに水沢公園のヒガン系桜群」いわての文化情報大事典（岩手県環境生活部若者女性協働推進室）]。一帯は「駒形神社及び水沢公園のヒガン系桜群」として岩手県指定天然記念物に指定されている。 File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) honden.JPG|本殿 File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) kaguraden.JPG|神楽殿 File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) shinmon.JPG|神門 === 奥宮・里宮 === * 奥宮 ** 鎮座地：駒ヶ岳山頂 *: 近世には「馬頭観音堂」と称されていた{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。水沢本社の新設までは本宮であったが、以後は奥宮として推移している{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。 * 雛小沢里宮（駒形神社） ** 鎮座地：岩手県[[胆沢郡]][[金ケ崎町]]西根雛子沢13 ** 祭神：国常立尊・大日孁貴尊・国狭槌尊・吾勝天主忍穂耳尊・彦火出見尊・置瀬尊[http://www.ganshinsei.jp/kanegasakichou/382a.html 駒形神社（雛小沢）]（岩手県神道青年会）。 ** 祭日：旧9月19日 == 摂末社 == 以下はいずれも本社境内社。 [[File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) Shiogama-jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|鹽竈神社（別宮）]] ; 別宮 :; [[鹽竈神社 (奥州市)|鹽竈神社]] ::* 祭神：鹽竈神（[[シオツチノオジ|塩土老翁神]]・[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌神]]・[[経津主神]]）、相殿に[[春日神]]（[[天児屋命|天児屋根神]]・[[比売神|比賣神]]・[[藤原鎌足|藤原鎌足朝臣]]） ::* 祭日：[[8月10日]] ::: 駒形神社本社の新設以前より、当地に鎮座した神社である。創建は不詳ながら、元々は石田・大明神の地にあったといい、一伝では[[康平]]5年（[[1062年]]）に[[源頼義]]・[[源義家|義家]]父子が石田・大明神の屋敷に社殿を造営し塩竈神を勧請したことに始まるという。勧請元の[[鹽竈神社]]（[[宮城県]][[塩竈市]]）は[[中世]]に[[留守氏]]の支配下にあったため、実際には[[寛文]]6年（[[1666年]]）に[[水沢城]]に入った[[留守宗利]]が勧請したことに始まると推測されている{{Sfn|塩竈神社（平凡社）|1990年}}。 ::: 一伝では、留守宗利が再興、[[明和]]3年（[[1766年]]）に現在地に遷座したという。[[安政]]6年（[[1859年]]）の水沢大火で社殿は焼失したが、[[文久]]2年（[[1862年]]）に再興。のち当地に駒形神社が移るにあたって社殿を譲り、境内摂社の春日神社に遷座した。明治の一時期は駒形神社とは分割される独立社であったが、その後は駒形神社の境内別宮として推移している{{Sfn|塩竈神社（平凡社）|1990年}}。 ; 末社 :; 山神社 ::* 祭神：[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫神]]・[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] ::* 祭日：[[4月12日]]・[[10月12日]] ; その他 :; 水沢招魂社 ::* 祭神：郷土出身の国事殉難者1099柱 ::* 祭日：[[6月2日]] ::: かつては水沢公園内に鎮座した。[[明治]]11年（[[1878年]]）の[[伊勢神宮]]分霊の巡行に際して、水沢公園内にあった愛宕神社（安政6年（1859年）の水沢大火で焼失）の跡地上に行在所が建てられたことに始まる。この行在所は明治42年（[[1909年]]）に招魂社に改められたが、その後荒廃し、駒形神社境内に遷座して現在に至っている{{Sfn|愛宕神社跡（平凡社）|1990年}}。 File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) Yama-jinja.JPG|山神社（末社） File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) Shokonsha.JPG|水沢招魂社 === 関係社 === * 岩崎の元里宮（駒形神社） ** 鎮座地：岩手県[[北上市]]和賀町岩崎18-25（{{Coord|39|16|58.47|N|141|2|50.78|E|region:JP-03_type:landmark|name=駒形神社（岩崎の元里宮）}}） ** 祭神：[[保食神]][http://www.ganshinsei.jp/kitakamishi/335a.html 駒形神社（岩崎）]（岩手県神道青年会）。 *: 水沢本社の新設までは里宮であったが、現在は本社との関係は絶たれている。古くは煤孫にあったが、のちに和賀に移されたという{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。 == 祭事 == * 月次祭 （毎月1日・19日）祭事の記載は神社由緒書による。 * 歳旦祭 （1月1日） * 祈年祭 （2月17日） * 奉遷記念大祭（春祭） （5月3日） * 招魂社大祭 （6月2日） * 大祓式（夏越の大祓） （6月30日） * 奥宮登拝祭 （8月1日） * 別宮塩釜神社祭（夏祭） （8月10日） * 例大祭（秋祭） （9月19日） * 末社山神社祭 （10月12日） * 新嘗祭（新穀感謝祭） （11月23日） * 大祓式（年越の大祓） （12月30日） * 除夜祭 （12月31日） == 文化財 == === 岩手県指定天然記念物 === * 駒形神社及び水沢公園のヒガン系桜群 - 昭和41年3月8日指定[https://www.city.oshu.iwate.jp/download.rbz?cmd=50&cd=2889&tg=20&inline=1 『奥州市の文化財』 pp. 90-107]より、p. 90。。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * 本社：[[岩手県]][[奥州市]]水沢中上野町1-83 * 奥宮：岩手県[[胆沢郡]][[金ケ崎町]]西根字駒ヶ岳（駒ヶ岳山頂） * 里宮：岩手県胆沢郡金ケ崎町西根雛子沢13 '''交通アクセス（水沢本社まで）''' * 鉄道：[[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）[[東北本線]] [[水沢駅]]（徒歩約13分） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書 * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[谷川健一]]編|author=小形信夫|year=1984|chapter=駒形神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 12 東北・北海道|publisher=[[白水社]]|isbn=4560022224|ref={{Harvid|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=虎尾俊哉・新野直吉|chapter=駒形神社|year=1986|title=式内社調査報告 第14巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|駒形神社（式内社）|1986年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 3 岩手県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=「駒ヶ岳」|ref={{Harvid|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「駒形神社」|ref={{Harvid|駒形神社（平凡社）|1990年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「塩竈神社」|ref={{Harvid|塩竈神社（平凡社）|1990年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「愛宕神社跡」|ref={{Harvid|愛宕神社跡（平凡社）|1990年}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1985|chapter=駒形神社|title=[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 3 岩手県|publisher=[[角川書店]]|isbn=4040010302|ref={{Harvid|駒形神社（角川）|1985年}}}} == 関連文献 == * 『[[古事類苑]]』 神宮司庁編、駒形神社項。 ** [{{NDLDC|1873551/470}} 『古事類苑 第9冊』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）470-471コマ参照。 * [[白井永二]]・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、146-147頁 * 『神道の本』[[Gakken|学研]]、1992年、212頁 == 関連項目 == * [[駒形根神社]] （[[宮城県]][[栗原市]]） - [[陸奥国]][[栗原郡]]の式内社。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Komagata shrine}} * [http://www.rnac.ne.jp/~komagata/ 駒形神社] - 公式サイト * [http://www.ganshinsei.jp/ousyuushi/371a.html 駒形神社（水沢）]、[http://www.ganshinsei.jp/kanegasakichou/382a.html 駒形神社（雛小沢）]、[http://www.ganshinsei.jp/kitakamishi/335a.html 駒形神社（岩崎）] - 岩手県神道青年会 * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/271101.html 駒形神社] 本社 拝殿 |所在地 = 本社：[[岩手県]][[奥州市]]水沢中上野町1-83
奥宮：岩手県[[胆沢郡]][[金ケ崎町]]西根字駒ヶ岳
里宮：岩手県胆沢郡金ケ崎町西根雛子沢13 |位置=本社：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|39|8|11.5|N|141|8|17.1|E|display=inline,title}}
奥宮：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|39|11|43.8|N|140|55|11.4|E|display=inline}}
里宮：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|39|11|8.9|N|141|3|42.7|E|display=inline}} |祭神 = 駒形大神
（[[天照大御神]]・[[天之常立神|天常立尊]]・[[国狭槌尊|国狭立尊]]・[[アメノオシホミミ|吾勝尊]]・置瀬尊・[[ホオリ|彦火尊]]の6柱の総称） |神体 = 駒ヶ岳（[[神体山]]） |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[国幣小社]]
[[別表神社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月19日]] |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map+|Japan Iwate|width=210|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Iwate|lat_deg=39|lat_min=8|lat_sec=11.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=8|lon_sec=17.1|position=bottom|label=本社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Iwate|lat_deg=39|lat_min=11|lat_sec=43.8|lon_deg=140|lon_min=55|lon_sec=11.4|position=left|label=奥宮|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Iwate|lat_deg=39|lat_min=11|lat_sec=8.9|lon_deg=141|lon_min=3|lon_sec=42.7|position=top|label=里宮|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|39.162461|N|141.028618|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=10|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|39|8|11.5|N|141|8|17.1|E}}|label1=本社|label-pos1=bottom|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|39|11|43.8|N|140|55|11.4|E}}|label2=奥宮|label-pos2=bottom|mark-size2=8|mark-coord3={{coord|39|11|8.9|N|141|3|42.7|E}}|label3=里宮|label-pos3=bottom|mark-size3=8}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''駒形神社'''（こまがたじんじゃ）は、[[岩手県]]にある[[神社]]。[[奥州市]]水沢中上野町の本社、[[胆沢郡]][[金ケ崎町]]西根の駒ヶ岳山頂の奥宮、胆沢郡金ケ崎町西根雛子沢の里宮からなる。[[式内社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[国幣小社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の6柱で、「'''駒形大神'''」と総称される神社由緒書。。 * [[天照大御神]] （あまてらすおおみかみ） * [[天之常立神|天常立尊]] （あめのとこたちのみこと） * [[国狭槌尊|国狭立尊]] （くにのさたちのみこと） * [[アメノオシホミミ|吾勝尊]] （あかつのみこと） * [[邇邇芸命|置瀬尊]] （おきせのみこと） * [[ホオリ|彦火尊]] （ひこほのみこと） 当社は駒ヶ岳の神霊を祀ったものとされるが、古くよりその神霊を人格神に比定する諸説が挙げられている。現在の6柱とする説は、雛子沢里宮の[[寛政]]9年（[[1797年]]）棟札や、[[仙台藩]]編纂の『安永風土記』に記載が見られる。これらは、中近世の神仏混交期において駒ヶ岳外輪山を各天神地祇に擬したことに由来すると見られている（最高峰の大日岳に天照大神、第2の駒ヶ岳に天照大神の子の吾勝尊、など）{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。以上のほか、人格神を[[ウガノミタマ|宇賀御魂大神]]・[[天照大神]]・[[アメノオシホミミ|天忍穂耳尊]]とする説、[[上毛野氏|毛野氏]]の祖神とする説等がある{{Sfn|駒形神社（平凡社）|1990年}}。 祭神の駒形神は[[ウマ|馬]]の守護神とされ、[[馬頭観音]]や[[大日如来]]と習合し、東日本の各地に[[勧請]]されて信仰されている{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。馬の守護神とされた背景は、古代に付近一帯が[[軍馬]]の産地であったことが考えられている{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。 == 歴史 == 当社は駒ヶ岳（[[焼石駒ヶ岳]]/駒形山）を祀る神社として、明治以前は駒ヶ岳山頂の本宮（奥宮）、[[北上市]]和賀町岩崎と[[金ケ崎町]]西根雛小沢の各里宮をして奉斎された{{Sfn|駒形神社（角川）|1985年}}。水沢の現在社（水沢本社）は[[明治]]36年（[[1903年]]）の新設である{{Sfn|駒形神社（平凡社）|1990年}}。それ以前の水沢本社の地は[[鹽竈神社 (奥州市)|鹽竈神社]]（現・境内別宮）の境内地であった{{Sfn|駒形神社（平凡社）|1990年}}。以下、駒形神社一般の歴史について概説する。 === 創建 === 創建は不詳で、様々な伝承が現在に伝えられている{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。社伝の1つでは、[[雄略天皇]]（第21代）21年に、[[籠神社]]（[[京都府]][[宮津市]]）から[[ウカノミタマ|宇賀御魂大神]]を勧請して山頂に祀り、里宮に[[オオゲツヒメ|大宜津比売神]]と[[事代主|事代主神]]を配祀したが、のちに前記6柱となったという{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。 別伝では、[[景行天皇]]（第12代）40年に[[日本武尊]]が東征に際し、[[蝦夷]]平定のために前記6柱を勧請・創建したという{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。また、[[坂上田村麻呂]]が当地で倒れた愛馬を祀ったが、のちに慈覚大師（[[円仁]]）が廻国した際にその駒形神を駒ヶ岳山頂に移して本宮を造営、さらに[[源義家]]が[[前九年の役]]の際に戦勝祈願をしたともいう{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。 別説として、上毛野（のちの[[上野国]]、現・[[群馬県]]）を根拠とする[[上毛野氏]]一族が当地に来住するにあたり、駒ヶ岳を上毛野氏氏神の[[赤城山]]（[[赤城神社]]）に擬して奉斎したとする説もある{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}{{Sfn|駒形神社（神々）|1984年}}。その中で、休火山である赤城山の外輪山に「駒形山」が存在することから、毛野氏が上毛野氏と下毛野氏に分かれた後にそれぞれ勢力を北に伸ばし、外輪山を持つ山の中で二番目の高峰を赤城山になぞらえて「駒ヶ岳」または「駒形山」と名付け、駒形大神を祀ったとする[http://www.rnac.ne.jp/~komagata/page002.html 「駒形神社御由緒」（駒形神社公式ホームページ）]。そして[[雄略天皇]]の時代に、上毛野氏が奥州において現在の「駒ヶ岳」を見出して名づけ、山頂に駒形大神を勧請したのが始まりであるとするものである。この説に従えば、駒形神社の祭神は赤城山の神と同一か、深い関連を持つ存在ということになる。関連して、『[[続日本後紀]]』[[承和 (日本)|承和]]8年（[[841年]]）3月2日条には江刺郡擬大領として「上毛野胆沢公毛人」の名が見え、上毛野氏と胆沢との関わりが指摘される{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。 さらに赤城神社との関連では、源実朝が『金塊和歌集』において赤城神社を「からやしろ」と詠んでいることから、赤城神社は「から」すなわち中国や韓国に由来するともされるが、当時の朝鮮にあった高麗が「コマ」と呼ばれたことから、駒形という名称は、「高麗唐」すなわち「コマカラ」が「こまかた」のち「こまがた」に転訛したともいう。関連して箱根神社の摂社である駒形神社では、朝鮮から高麗大神が勧請されたとしている。 なお奥宮は現在駒ヶ岳に鎮座するが、元々は駒ヶ岳南方で最高峰の大日岳（経塚山）山頂にあったとする伝承がある{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。 === 概史 === [[六国史|国史]]では[[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）に「駒形神」の[[神階]]が正五位下に、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]4年（[[862年]]）に従四位下に昇叙された旨の記事が見える{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。従四位下の神階は[[陸奥国]]内で最高位になる（陸奥国内で従四位下は計9社陸奥国で六国史終了時点で従四位下まで進んだ神社としては、駒形神社のほか、苅田嶺神社（論社複数）、[[鹽竈神社|志波彦神社]]、[[零羊崎神社 (石巻市湊)|零羊埼神社]]、[[拝幣志神社]]、計仙麻神社（論社複数）、[[日高見神社 (石巻市)|日高見神社]]、志波姫神社（論社複数）、[[磐椅神社]]の計9社がある。なお東北地方で見た場合には、[[出羽国]]において[[大物忌神社]]と[[月山神社]]が各々従二位に叙せられている。）[http://www.ganshinsei.jp/ousyuushi/371a.html 「駒形神社」（岩手県神道青年会ホームページ）]。駒形神社側ではこの神階について、駒形神社への坂上田村麻呂の崇敬が篤かった関係で、胆沢の鎮守府から神階を高くすべきとの申し出が何度もあったためと説明している。 また、[[延長 (元号)|延長]]5年（[[927年]]）成立の『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]では陸奥国[[胆沢郡]]に「駒形神社」と記載され、[[式内社]]に列している{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。駒形神社側の見解では、全国の駒形神社の中で当社と宮城県栗原郡の駒形根神社の2社のみが神名帳に記載があるという。 平安時代頃には、上記の坂上田村麻呂のほか、源頼義・義家や[[平泉]]の藤原氏の崇敬も篤かったと伝える。箱根神社の縁起には、藤原秀衡が銅を鋳て駒形の神の像を作り祀るなど、藤原氏の駒形大神への信仰の篤さが読み取れる記載があるという。 [[江戸時代]]の時点では、駒ヶ岳は[[仙台藩]]と[[盛岡藩]]の境界であり、里宮はそれぞれの藩内に1社ずつがあった{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。また、山頂の本宮（奥宮）は両藩によって20年目ごとに交互に建て替えがなされていた{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）、山頂の本宮（奥宮）が[[近代社格制度]]において[[国幣小社]]に列するにあたり、本宮・里宮とも参拝に不便であるとして、当時の[[水沢県]]県庁に近い鹽竈神社の本殿が仮遥拝所となされた（鹽竈神社は境内社の春日神社に遷座）{{Sfn|駒形神社（平凡社）|1990年}}。そして明治7年（[[1874年]]）、社殿が改修されて正式な遥拝所とされた。明治36年（[[1903年]]）に山頂の神霊が遥拝所に遷され、元は鹽竈神社のものであった社殿等一切は駒形神社に編入された。鹽竈神社は境内別宮であった春日神社に合祀され、社名が「春日神社」から「鹽竈神社」に改称されることとなった。 戦後は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に列し、近年では[[陸中国]][[一宮]]とも称されている。 2010年（平成22年）8月1日、山頂の奥宮にある老朽化した社殿を解体し、新しく社殿を造営した[http://jinja.blog5.fc2.com/blog-entry-465.html 「8月1日、奥宮竣工祭、登拝祭」駒形神社なんでも綴り（駒形神社宮司・山下明のブログ）]。 === 神階 === * [[仁寿]]元年（[[851年]]）9月2日、正五位下 （『[[日本文徳天皇実録]]』） - 表記は「駒形神」。 * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]4年（[[862年]]）6月18日、正五位下から従四位下 （『[[日本三代実録]]』） - 表記は「駒形神」。 == 境内 == === 本社 === 境内と[[水沢公園]]はヒガンザクラ系の桜の名所として知られ、老木の樹齢は250年から300年にも及ぶ{{Sfn|駒形神社（平凡社）|1990年}}[http://www.bunka.pref.iwate.jp/rekishi/bunkazai/index.html 「駒形神社ならびに水沢公園のヒガン系桜群」いわての文化情報大事典（岩手県環境生活部若者女性協働推進室）]。一帯は「駒形神社及び水沢公園のヒガン系桜群」として岩手県指定天然記念物に指定されている。 File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) honden.JPG|本殿 File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) kaguraden.JPG|神楽殿 File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) shinmon.JPG|神門 === 奥宮・里宮 === * 奥宮 ** 鎮座地：駒ヶ岳山頂 *: 近世には「馬頭観音堂」と称されていた{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。水沢本社の新設までは本宮であったが、以後は奥宮として推移している{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。 * 雛小沢里宮（駒形神社） ** 鎮座地：岩手県[[胆沢郡]][[金ケ崎町]]西根雛子沢13 ** 祭神：国常立尊・大日孁貴尊・国狭槌尊・吾勝天主忍穂耳尊・彦火出見尊・置瀬尊[http://www.ganshinsei.jp/kanegasakichou/382a.html 駒形神社（雛小沢）]（岩手県神道青年会）。 ** 祭日：旧9月19日 == 摂末社 == 以下はいずれも本社境内社。 [[File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) Shiogama-jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|鹽竈神社（別宮）]] ; 別宮 :; [[鹽竈神社 (奥州市)|鹽竈神社]] ::* 祭神：鹽竈神（[[シオツチノオジ|塩土老翁神]]・[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌神]]・[[経津主神]]）、相殿に[[春日神]]（[[天児屋命|天児屋根神]]・[[比売神|比賣神]]・[[藤原鎌足|藤原鎌足朝臣]]） ::* 祭日：[[8月10日]] ::: 駒形神社本社の新設以前より、当地に鎮座した神社である。創建は不詳ながら、元々は石田・大明神の地にあったといい、一伝では[[康平]]5年（[[1062年]]）に[[源頼義]]・[[源義家|義家]]父子が石田・大明神の屋敷に社殿を造営し塩竈神を勧請したことに始まるという。勧請元の[[鹽竈神社]]（[[宮城県]][[塩竈市]]）は[[中世]]に[[留守氏]]の支配下にあったため、実際には[[寛文]]6年（[[1666年]]）に[[水沢城]]に入った[[留守宗利]]が勧請したことに始まると推測されている{{Sfn|塩竈神社（平凡社）|1990年}}。 ::: 一伝では、留守宗利が再興、[[明和]]3年（[[1766年]]）に現在地に遷座したという。[[安政]]6年（[[1859年]]）の水沢大火で社殿は焼失したが、[[文久]]2年（[[1862年]]）に再興。のち当地に駒形神社が移るにあたって社殿を譲り、境内摂社の春日神社に遷座した。明治の一時期は駒形神社とは分割される独立社であったが、その後は駒形神社の境内別宮として推移している{{Sfn|塩竈神社（平凡社）|1990年}}。 ; 末社 :; 山神社 ::* 祭神：[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫神]]・[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]] ::* 祭日：[[4月12日]]・[[10月12日]] ; その他 :; 水沢招魂社 ::* 祭神：郷土出身の国事殉難者1099柱 ::* 祭日：[[6月2日]] ::: かつては水沢公園内に鎮座した。[[明治]]11年（[[1878年]]）の[[伊勢神宮]]分霊の巡行に際して、水沢公園内にあった愛宕神社（安政6年（1859年）の水沢大火で焼失）の跡地上に行在所が建てられたことに始まる。この行在所は明治42年（[[1909年]]）に招魂社に改められたが、その後荒廃し、駒形神社境内に遷座して現在に至っている{{Sfn|愛宕神社跡（平凡社）|1990年}}。 File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) Yama-jinja.JPG|山神社（末社） File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) Shokonsha.JPG|水沢招魂社 === 関係社 === * 岩崎の元里宮（駒形神社） ** 鎮座地：岩手県[[北上市]]和賀町岩崎18-25（{{Coord|39|16|58.47|N|141|2|50.78|E|region:JP-03_type:landmark|name=駒形神社（岩崎の元里宮）}}） ** 祭神：[[保食神]][http://www.ganshinsei.jp/kitakamishi/335a.html 駒形神社（岩崎）]（岩手県神道青年会）。 *: 水沢本社の新設までは里宮であったが、現在は本社との関係は絶たれている。古くは煤孫にあったが、のちに和賀に移されたという{{Sfn|駒ヶ岳（平凡社）|1990年}}。 == 祭事 == * 月次祭 （毎月1日・19日）祭事の記載は神社由緒書による。 * 歳旦祭 （1月1日） * 祈年祭 （2月17日） * 奉遷記念大祭（春祭） （5月3日） * 招魂社大祭 （6月2日） * 大祓式（夏越の大祓） （6月30日） * 奥宮登拝祭 （8月1日） * 別宮塩釜神社祭（夏祭） （8月10日） * 例大祭（秋祭） （9月19日） * 末社山神社祭 （10月12日） * 新嘗祭（新穀感謝祭） （11月23日） * 大祓式（年越の大祓） （12月30日） * 除夜祭 （12月31日） == 文化財 == === 岩手県指定天然記念物 === * 駒形神社及び水沢公園のヒガン系桜群 - 昭和41年3月8日指定[https://www.city.oshu.iwate.jp/download.rbz?cmd=50&cd=2889&tg=20&inline=1 『奥州市の文化財』 pp. 90-107]より、p. 90。。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * 本社：[[岩手県]][[奥州市]]水沢中上野町1-83 * 奥宮：岩手県[[胆沢郡]][[金ケ崎町]]西根字駒ヶ岳（駒ヶ岳山頂） * 里宮：岩手県胆沢郡金ケ崎町西根雛子沢13 '''交通アクセス（水沢本社まで）''' * 鉄道：[[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）[[東北本線]] [[水沢駅]]（徒歩約13分） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''注釈''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''出典''' {{reflist|2}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Komagata-jinja (Oshu) torii.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of [[Rikuchū Province]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the [[Hakone]] region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times [[Horses in warfare|war horses]] were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[industrial development]] and [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with [[Academic achievement|exam success]] and [[home safety]]. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Guanyin|Kannon]] and [[Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]]. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the [[Hakone]] region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times [[Horses in warfare|war horses]] were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Hikohohodemi no Mikoto|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[industrial development]] and [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with [[Academic achievement|exam success]] and [[home safety]]. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Guanyin|Kannon]] and [[Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]]. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Komagata-jinja (Oshu) torii.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of [[Rikuchū Province]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the [[Hakone]] region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times [[Horses in warfare|war horses]] were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu]] and [[Hoori|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[industrial development]] and [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with [[Academic achievement|exam success]] and [[home safety]]. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Guanyin|Kannon]] and [[Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]]. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

[[sn:Komagata Shrine]] [[en:Komagata Shrine]] [[ja:%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:奥州市の建築物]] [[Category:奥州市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:金ケ崎町の建築物
revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Komagata-jinja (Oshu) torii.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of [[Rikuchū Province]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the [[Hakone]] region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times [[Horses in warfare|war horses]] were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu]] and [[Hoori|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[industrial development]] and [[Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with [[Academic achievement|exam success]] and [[home safety]]. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Kannon|Kannon]] and [[Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]]. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
== References == {{reflist}} {{coord|35.18746|139.02162|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinmei shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \
{{Ichinomiya}}
{{translated page|ja|駒形神社|version=100887751|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
