<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11668370]] Wikidata name: Komagata Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) haiden.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance | prefecture= {{ill|Oshu|WD=Q386052}} | coordinates= {{Coord|39.136553|N|141.138086|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of [[:en:Rikuchū Province|Rikuchū Province]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the [[:en:Hakone|Hakone]] region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times [[:en:Horses in warfare|war horses]] were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu]] and [[Hoori|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[:en:Industrial development|Industrial development]] and [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with [[:en:Academic achievement|exam success]] and [[:en:Home safety|Home safety]]. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[:en:Wish|Wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[:en:Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Kannon|Kannon]] and [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]]. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[:en:Shiogama shrine|Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11668370]] Wikidata name: Komagata Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) haiden.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance | prefecture= {{ill|Oshu|WD=Q386052}} | coordinates= {{Coord|39.136553|N|141.138086|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Japan|Japan]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of [[:en:Rikuchū Province|Rikuchū Province]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the [[:en:Hakone|Hakone]] region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times [[:en:Horses in warfare|war horses]] were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu]] and [[Hoori|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[:en:Industrial development|Industrial development]] and [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with [[:en:Academic achievement|exam success]] and [[:en:Home safety|Home safety]]. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal [[:en:Wish|Wish]]es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like [[:en:Shiogama shrine|Shiogama Jinja]] and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mt. Akagi]]. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Kannon|Kannon]] and [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]]. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of [[:en:Shiogama shrine|Shiogama shrine]] but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
== References == {{reflist}} {{coord|35.18746|139.02162|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinmei shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \
{{Ichinomiya}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11668370]] Wikidata name: Komagata Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:19, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) haiden.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance | prefecture= {{ill|Oshu|WD=Q386052}} | coordinates= {{Coord|39.136553|N|141.138086|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Rikuchū Province|en|Rikuchū Province|WD=Q1192262|lt=Rikuchū Province}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the {{ill|Hakone|en|Hakone|WD=Q671040|lt=Hakone}} region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times {{ill|Horses in warfare|en|Horses in warfare|WD=Q1194462|lt=war horses}} were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu]] and [[Hoori|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[:en:Industrial development|Industrial development]] and [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with {{ill|Academic achievement|en|Academic achievement|WD=Q1815842|lt=exam success}} and {{ill|Home safety|en|Home safety|WD=Q15129485|lt=Home safety}}. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal {{ill|Wish|en|Wish|WD=Q241625|lt=Wish}}es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Jinja}} and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mt. Akagi}}. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Kannon|Kannon]] and {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai}}. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama shrine}} but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama shrine}} but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]]
{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) haiden.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance | prefecture= {{ill|Oshu|WD=Q386052}} | coordinates= {{Coord|39.136553|N|141.138086|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Rikuchū Province|en|Rikuchū Province|WD=Q1192262|lt=Rikuchū Province}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the {{ill|Hakone|en|Hakone|WD=Q671040|lt=Hakone}} region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times {{ill|Horses in warfare|en|Horses in warfare|WD=Q1194462|lt=war horses}} were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu]] and [[Hoori|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[:en:Industrial development|Industrial development]] and [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with {{ill|Academic achievement|en|Academic achievement|WD=Q1815842|lt=exam success}} and {{ill|Home safety|en|Home safety|WD=Q15129485|lt=Home safety}}. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal {{ill|Wish|en|Wish|WD=Q241625|lt=Wish}}es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Jinja}} and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mt. Akagi}}. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Kannon|Kannon]] and {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai}}. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama shrine}} but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama shrine}} but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]]
{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) haiden.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance | prefecture= {{ill|Oshu|WD=Q386052}} | coordinates= {{Coord|39.136553|N|141.138086|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Rikuchū Province|en|Rikuchū Province|WD=Q1192262|lt=Rikuchū Province}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the {{ill|Hakone|en|Hakone|WD=Q671040|lt=Hakone}} region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times {{ill|Horses in warfare|en|Horses in warfare|WD=Q1194462|lt=war horses}} were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu]] and [[Hoori|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[:en:Industrial development|Industrial development]] and [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with {{ill|Academic achievement|en|Academic achievement|WD=Q1815842|lt=exam success}} and {{ill|Home safety|en|Home safety|WD=Q15129485|lt=Home safety}}. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal {{ill|Wish|en|Wish|WD=Q241625|lt=Wish}}es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Jinja}} and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mt. Akagi}}. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Kannon|Kannon]] and {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai}}. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama shrine}} but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
== References == {{reflist}} {{coord|35.18746|139.02162|format=dms|type:landmark_region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinmei shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \
{{Ichinomiya}}

It features the horse head [[Kannon|Kannon]] and {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|1=Vairocana}}. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]]
{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) haiden.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance | prefecture= {{ill|Oshu|WD=Q386052|1=Ōshū, Iwate}} | coordinates= {{Coord|39.136553|N|141.138086|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Rikuchū Province|en|Rikuchū Province|WD=Q1192262|lt=Rikuchū Province|1=Rikuchū Province}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the {{ill|Hakone|en|Hakone|WD=Q671040|lt=Hakone|1=Hakone}} region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times {{ill|Horses in warfare|en|Horses in warfare|WD=Q1194462|lt=war horses|1=Horses in warfare}} were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu]] and [[Hoori|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[:en:Industrial development|Industrial development]] and [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with {{ill|Academic achievement|en|Academic achievement|WD=Q1815842|lt=exam success|1=Academic achievement}} and {{ill|Home safety|en|Home safety|WD=Q15129485|lt=Home safety|1=Home safety}}. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal {{ill|Wish|en|Wish|WD=Q241625|lt=Wish|1=Wish}}es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Jinja|1=Shiogama shrine}} and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mt. Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}}. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Kannon|Kannon]] and {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|1=Vairocana}}. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Kannon|Kannon]] and {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|1=Vairocana}}. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Komagata shrine]][[en:Komagata Shrine]][[ja:駒形神社]][[simple:Komagata Shrine]]
{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) haiden.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance | prefecture= {{ill|Oshu|WD=Q386052|1=Ōshū, Iwate}} | coordinates= {{Coord|39.136553|N|141.138086|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Rikuchū Province|en|Rikuchū Province|WD=Q1192262|lt=Rikuchū Province|1=Rikuchū Province}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the {{ill|Hakone|en|Hakone|WD=Q671040|lt=Hakone|1=Hakone}} region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times {{ill|Horses in warfare|en|Horses in warfare|WD=Q1194462|lt=war horses|1=Horses in warfare}} were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu]] and [[Hoori|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[:en:Industrial development|Industrial development]] and [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with {{ill|Academic achievement|en|Academic achievement|WD=Q1815842|lt=exam success|1=Academic achievement}} and {{ill|Home safety|en|Home safety|WD=Q15129485|lt=Home safety|1=Home safety}}. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal {{ill|Wish|en|Wish|WD=Q241625|lt=Wish|1=Wish}}es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Jinja|1=Shiogama shrine}} and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mt. Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}}. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Kannon|Kannon]] and {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|1=Vairocana}}. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|WD=Q133753|lt=Shiogama shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[:en:Industrial development|Industrial development]] and [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with {{ill|Academic achievement|en|Academic achievement|qid=Q1815842|lt=exam success|1=Academic achievement}} and {{ill|Home safety|en|Home safety|qid=Q15129485|lt=Home safety|1=Home safety}}. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal {{ill|Wish|en|Wish|qid=Q241625|lt=Wish|1=Wish}}es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|qid=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Jinja|1=Shiogama shrine}} and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|qid=Q415251|lt=Mt. Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}}. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Kannon|Kannon]] and {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|qid=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|1=Vairocana}}. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|qid=Q133753|lt=Shiogama shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Komagata-jinja (Oshu) haiden.JPG | caption = Torii at the entrance | prefecture= {{ill|Oshu|qid=Q386052|1=Ōshū, Iwate}} | coordinates= {{Coord|39.136553|N|141.138086|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Komagata Shrine''' is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Seven Wonders of Tainaisan Shrine - Hanamaki, Iwate |url=https://en.japantravel.com/iwate/the-seven-wonders-of-tainaisan-shrine/66434 |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=JapanTravel |date=27 March 2021 |language=en}}</ref><ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" /> It is the [[ichinomiya]] of {{ill|Rikuchū Province|en|Rikuchū Province|qid=Q1192262|lt=Rikuchū Province|1=Rikuchū Province}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Komagata Shrine attraction reviews - Komagata Shrine tickets - Komagata Shrine discounts - Komagata Shrine transportation, address, opening hours - attractions, hotels, and food near Komagata Shrine |url=https://www.trip.com/travel-guide/attraction/oshu-shi/komagata-shrine-58333827/ |access-date=2023-04-25 |website=TRIP.COM |language=en-XX}}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is called "Komagata-san" by locals and is a significant religious place in the {{ill|Hakone|en|Hakone|qid=Q671040|lt=Hakone|1=Hakone}} region.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a">{{Cite web |title=Hakone-Sekisho |url=https://www.hakonesekisyo.jp/english/data/around/around06.html |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=www.hakonesekisyo.jp}}</ref>
== Overview == The entrance to the shrine has a Koshin pagoda and red banners that represent the seven deities of Hakone. Stones at the entrance show the Buddhist demon-god, Shomen Kongo. Stairs lead to the main shrine.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
In ancient times {{ill|Horses in warfare|en|Horses in warfare|qid=Q1194462|lt=war horses|1=Horses in warfare}} were grown there.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a">{{Cite web |date=2023-05-02 |title=駒形神社 本社（komagata shrine） - shrine-heritager |url=https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |access-date=2023-11-21 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230502015657/https://shrineheritager.com/en/%E9%A7%92%E5%BD%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-%E6%9C%AC%E7%A4%BE%EF%BC%88komagata-shrine%EF%BC%89/ |archive-date=2023-05-02 }}</ref>
Komagata Shrine is important in Japan. It is known as "Riku Chugoku [[Ichinomiya]]". The inner shrine, "Okumiya", is on [[Komagatake (Iwate Prefecture)|Mount Komagatake]] summit. The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head Kannon and Dainichi Nyorai. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Deities worshipped == The shrine is dedicated to six gods. These include [[Amaterasu]] and [[Hoori|Hiko Hohodemi no Mikoto]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine offers various blessings. These include [[:en:Industrial development|Industrial development]] and [[:en:Road traffic safety|traffic safety]]. It helps with {{ill|Academic achievement|en|Academic achievement|qid=Q1815842|lt=exam success|1=Academic achievement}} and {{ill|Home safety|en|Home safety|qid=Q15129485|lt=Home safety|1=Home safety}}. It also offers protection against bad directions. The shrine fulfills personal {{ill|Wish|en|Wish|qid=Q241625|lt=Wish|1=Wish}}es.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
Other deities are also in the precincts. Yama no Kamisha includes [[Ōyamatsumi|Oyamatsumi no Kami]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohanasakuya Hime no Kami]]. There are separate shrines like {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|qid=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Jinja|1=Shiogama shrine}} and [[Mizusawa Shokonsha]].<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. Many legends are associated with it. The [[Kameno clan]] revered {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|qid=Q415251|lt=Mt. Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}}. They worshiped the god of Akagi. The worship evolved over time. [[Komagata Ogami]] was enshrined on Komagatake. The shrine appears in the "[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]". This record was completed in December 927 AD.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine appears in "Nihon Montokutenno Jitsuroku" and "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]].".<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The area around the shrine was once known for war horse production. The shrine is linked to Shinto Buddhist studies. It features the horse head [[Kannon|Kannon]] and {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|qid=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|1=Vairocana}}. People worship it in many parts of Eastern Japan.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
The shrine used to be a [[subshrine]] of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|qid=Q133753|lt=Shiogama shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} but it later was moved since it was hard to get to.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Okumiya and Satomiya == Originally, Okumiya was on "Dainichidake." It was later moved to "Komagatake." Satomiya are auxiliary shrines. They were built at Komagatake's foot. This made worship easier.<ref name="駒形神社-本社-2023a" />
== Sub-temples and deities == The shrine complex has different [[Setsumatsusha|subshrines]]:<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
* Bishamondo: A sub-shrine for one of the seven Hakone deities.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Inuzuka Myojin: A sub-shrine linked to old Hakone inns.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" /> * Minokasa Myojin: A sub-shrine connected to fish harvests from Lake Ashi.<ref name="wwwhakonesekisyojp-2023a" />
